Scenario.title: Energy Sector Disruption via Web App Exploitation
Scenario.overview: Energy Intermediate The attacker targets vulnerable web applications in the energy sector to gain initial access, then moves laterally and escalates privileges before deploying disruptive malware to industrial control systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing Assets, Domain Controller, Memory, Industrial Control Systems (ICS), Compromised Server, Compromised System
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Scripting Language, Legitimate Tools, Credential Access, Upload, Vulnerability Exploit, Supply Chain Compromise

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker exploits a vulnerability in an internet-facing web application, likely a SCADA system or energy trading platform, using alternate encoding to bypass input validation.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker uses built-in tools and scripts to perform tasks on the compromised system, avoiding detection by blending in with legitimate activity.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: The attacker uploads a web shell to a compromised server, allowing them to maintain access and execute commands remotely.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: The attacker steals credentials from the domain controller and uses them to escalate privileges within the network.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attacker employs fileless malware written in a scripting language to avoid traditional antivirus detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker introduces malware into industrial control systems via a supply chain compromise, causing disruption or damage to energy production processes.

Scenario.summary: The C0017 group targets the energy sector by exploiting vulnerabilities in web applications, moving laterally, and escalating privileges before deploying disruptive malware to critical infrastructure, leading to potential disruptions in energy production.
